@[100063507584714:2048:Chico News & Review]'s Best of Chico special edition is celebrated for showcasing the extraordinary individuals and businesses who infuse our community with vitality and compassion. The nominations of Charles Withuhn, Addison Winslow, and Ashley-Michelle Papon for Best Activist exemplify Chico's unique spirit, where passionate citizens drive real, impactful change. Let’s get to know these nominees better so that you can cast your vote proudly–voting, after all, is crucial in honoring these champions of progress. Cast your vote: https://bestofchico.newsreview.com/community

🌟 **𝐀𝐝𝐝𝐢𝐬𝐨𝐧 𝐖𝐢𝐧𝐬𝐥𝐨𝐰: 𝐁𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐀𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐬𝐭 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐁𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐋𝐨𝐜𝐚𝐥 𝐎𝐟𝐟𝐢𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭** 🌟

Chico’s favorite son, @[100084255104910:2048:Councilmember Addison Winslow] has journeyed from passionate activist to City Council member and his work is nothing short of transformative. With nearly a decade of dedication behind the scenes, Winslow has reshaped Chico’s landscape with his progressive vision.

From leading vital mutual aid during the pandemic to championing sustainable policies and affordable housing, Winslow’s impact is profound. His innovative approach to "compact urban growth" and unwavering commitment to equity set a high bar for community activism.

As a finalist for Best Activist, Winslow embodies the spirit of change and resilience, driving Chico toward a brighter, more inclusive future. 💪🏼 

Learn more about Addison and his work here: https://www.standupforchico.com/news/chico-news-and-review-best-of-chico-best-activist-and-best-city-official-nominee-addison-winslow

Highly Anticipated Book Release by Quacy L. Smith!!!! 
We live in an era of exceptional challenges—climate crises, widening social divides, economic instability, and political unrest. The Challenge We Face unpacks these complex issues in a way that's accessible and engaging for everyone. With stories from around the world, the author connects the dots between these global problems and our everyday lives, revealing how deeply intertwined they are. But this book doesn't just diagnose the issues; it offers a vision of hope and actionable steps to create change. Whether you're new to these topics or have been following them for years, The Challenge We Face invites you to see the world differently and join the growing movement of people committed to building a better future. Available for purchase on Amazon, Barnes & Noble, and other booksellers.

When Kim Konikow set out to find her forever home, her search ended in Fergus Falls — a community that "just felt right." Drawn by its vibrant arts scene and welcoming spirit, Kim now shares her own cultural traditions with her new neighbors. 

Through events like “Latkes and Hanukkah Traditions,” she invites the community to connect, cook, and celebrate together. “Sharing our cultures lessens the fear of the unknown,” she says. “It helps us be more welcoming.”

ad_text:
Building Hope, Building Community.

Our Hope Builders are a dedicated group of compassionate supporters of Habitat St. Johns, like you, who are making a tangible difference in the lives of families in St. Johns County every month.

As a Hope Builder, your ongoing, automatic monthly contribution provides a reliable source of funding that allows Habitat St. Johns to plan long-term projects, respond to urgent needs, and budget more effectively, thereby strengthening your impact.

This consistent support is crucial for creating sustainable, life-changing outcomes—helping families move from instability to security and from uncertainty to hope. By becoming a Hope Builder, you’re not just donating; you’re investing in the future of these families.

Join our Hope Builders at https://habitatstjohns.charityproud.org/GiveMonthly.

Thank you, for all you do to help us make a difference in our community!

ad_text:
💫 Exciting Announcement! 💫

We are proud to introduce Valencia Gunder @[17841401627592865:6057:@valenciagunder] as our Black Prides United 2025 Grand Marshal! 🎉

A proud multi-generational resident of Miami’s Liberty City, Valencia is a trailblazer dedicated to community empowerment and social justice. She serves as co-director of The Smile Trust @[17841401497893386:6057:@smiletrustinc] , executive director of The Black Collective and The Freedom Lab, and founder of the Community Emergency Operations Center — organizations that reach across the US South and the Caribbean from their home base in Liberty City.

For over 15 years, Valencia has been a vital force supporting low-wealth communities facing climate change impacts. Her leadership has facilitated over 500,000 wellness checks and the distribution of $7 million in supplies to those in need across the US South and the Caribbean.

Black Prides United 2025 takes place October 9-12!
Join us on Saturday, October 11, at Esplanade Park in Fort Lauderdale, FL, for the Unity Block Festival, where Valencia will also be honored with Afro Pride’s Philanthropy Award.

Let’s come together to celebrate her incredible work and the vibrant Black community! 💙✊🏿

#BlackPridesUnited #GrandMarshal #UnityBlockFestival #Miami #FortLauderdale #LibertyCity #BlackExcellence #CommunityLeadership #AfroPride #Philanthropy #ClimateJustice

This is what happens when you let politics, not reality, dictate energy policy.

"The net result of German politicians’ shortsightedness in phasing out nuclear power is a vastly pricier grid. The new analysis shows that if Germans simply maintained their 2002 fleet of reactors through 2022, they could have saved themselves roughly $600 billion Euros. Why so much? Well, in addition to their construction costs, renewables required expensive grid upgrades and subsidies."

https://www.realclearscience.com/articles/2024/08/27/study_quantifies_germanys_disastrous_switch_away_from_nuclear_power_1053889.html

ad_text:
📐 Introducing Victoria Saracco — Our Newest 39er

If you've ever stood in the shadow of a building and felt the spirit of a city embedded in its walls, then you've already felt the kind of work Victoria believes in.

A born-and-raised Galvestonian, Victoria is an architectural designer whose passion for sustainable, human-scale design was shaped by childhood memories in her grandparents’ loft in the Old Daily News Building—a structure that weathered the 1900 storm, Hurricane Ike, and now, the next generation’s vision for Galveston’s future.

At just 25, she’s already thinking beyond blueprints. Victoria sees our island not as a relic or tourist stop, but as a place with untapped potential for live-work communities, revitalized commercial spaces, and design that reflects the soul of the people who live here—not just those who visit.

She's deeply rooted in Galveston's story—from MREs after the storm to over-the-head headphones at MOD—bringing both resilience and edge to every space she touches.

Welcome, Victoria. We're honored to have your voice, your vision, and your velocity as a part of the 39ers.

This is the new Galveston. And she is building it.

ad_text:
This is simply surreal. And whatever “national energy emergency” the administration is referring to is completely beyond us. In 1970, approximately 46% of US energy production came from burning coal. In 2025 it reached an all-time low of 15%. That’s 2% less than wind and solar. Florida has recently phased out virtually all of its former coal-burning power plants. Any new coal production in the US will almost certainly be for export to countries that still use coal for a large share of their energy production. And if you’re complaining about their emissions, you’re also complaining about the burning of US coal outside our country. Our planet only has one atmosphere.

We will do a follow-up post on recent changes the administration has made to regulations on things like coal ash and where the waste from coal mines can be dumped. This is a very sorry step in the wrong direction.

Our follow-up post will also include information on how emissions from coal plants elsewhere end up contributing large amounts of mercury to Florida’s ecosystems.

Hi my name is Josh Lucas and I'm proud to announce that I'm running to be the next Mayor of Fort Worth—From the perspective of someone who was born and raised in Fort Worth we are a world class city, but we don't have world class leadership.  I’m an uncle, a Democrat, and someone deeply committed to protecting our democracy and our community from the rising tide of right-wing extremism.Fort Worth is now the 11th largest city in the U.S. and the seat of Tarrant County. It’s time we start leading with compassion, equity, and a vision for progress.

As your mayor, I’ll fight to attract businesses that create real jobs with fair wages and benefits for our residents. But economic growth means little if we ignore the systemic issues harming our community. Since 2017, our county jail—just across the street from Tarrant County Community College—has reported 69 deaths while in custody, this is unacceptable. As your mayor I’ll advocate for a Mental Health Crisis Emergency Center as part of a robust jail diversion program, ensuring that vulnerable people are treated with care, not locked away indefinitely. 

We’ll also invest in our youth by reforming our Juvenile Justice system, centering programs around Fort Worth’s rich cultural arts. Every child deserves opportunities for growth, creativity, and success—regardless of their zip code or family income. And let’s not forget the basics: fixing potholes, improving public transit, and building sustainable infrastructure. These aren’t partisan issues—they’re about serving our community effectively. 

Democratic leadership means prioritizing people over profits, equity over exclusion, and progress over stagnation. When we lift up the most vulnerable among us, we create a stronger, more united Fort Worth.

Together, we can build a city that reflects our shared values—a city where everyone has the chance to thrive, and y'all really means all.

(ok I sound tired but it was almost midnight! 😴)

Housing is a human right, and I believe we have a shared responsibility to allow more of it to address this crisis.

Last week, City Council discussed Culver City’s proposed zoning changes for the General Plan. And there's a lot worth celebrating— the proposal makes many single use neighborhoods across the city into mixed use.

That means more housing near jobs, amenities, and local businesses! 🏪🏘️🚍

But the plan also adds most of our new housing capacity to our densest and most diverse neighborhood (Fox Hills) which has less transit and fewer local businesses in walking distance, while leaving several other neighborhoods untouched. 

Allowing for more housing is a shared responsibility, and we all have a role to play to keep Culver City a diverse and inclusive place for all!

While we might not be able to persuade your Uncle Jim, there are totally reasonable, non-MAGA voters out there who we can reach. We do it by telling them about Trump and the far-right’s Project 2025 plan to effectively ban access to abortion, gut veterans’ health services and disability benefits, and roll back climate initiatives, which could lead to billions of tons of extra pollution.

MVP is funding ground-game operations in battleground states to reach those voters, and your contributions support our efforts. Use the link below to pitch in now!

Happy World Ocean Day! 🌊🌊🌊

Today, we honor our Earth’s greatest gift: the ocean. It sustains life, makes up the majority of the planet, and inspires joy to people all around the world. But this precious resource is under threat from plastics, pollution, and the impacts of climate change. 

Now, more than ever, the ocean needs more friends. 🤝

Are you a friend? Celebrate #WorldOceanDay by donating to Surfrider and becoming an official Friend of the Ocean. Together, we can ensure our ocean is protected for generations to come. Link in bio.

🌊 Why is clean water one of our campaign points? Well, because Sparta’s water is at risk.

While our water quality is generally good, PFAS levels (Forever Chemicals) exceed the EPA limit by 2.5x. This poses serious health risks, and we need a plan to address and remove these harmful chemicals from our water supply.

Building a 770,000 sq. ft. warehouse just 15 feet above 45-70% of our water supply will only worsen the problem. The 10+ million truck tires rolling over the site each year will generate more PFAS pollution, further endangering our community’s water.

We need smart solutions for our water, not harmful development. 💧

Recent legislation may have made things more complex—but not less profitable. Developers across oil & gas, renewables, transmission, fiber, and data center infrastructure are still pouring into Texas. Why? Because demand here isn’t theoretical. It’s real. It’s growing. And it’s urgent.

The average American family spends $1,500 a year on food that doesn’t get eaten. You can learn to save money and the environment in your home and community at the FREE Save the Food Fest.

The Save the Food Fest will be hosted by Snohomish County WSU Extension’s Sustainable Community Stewards, Agricultural, and SNAP-ED programs from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. on Saturday, April 5th at the Rosehill Community Center in Mukilteo.

The event is free and open to all Snohomish County residents, though a donation to the Mukilteo Food Bank is highly encouraged.

Visitors can attend two workshops on food preservation, cooking with ends and stems, and creating a waste-free kitchen. The event also includes an art and recipe contest, raffles for cookbooks and kitchen supplies, an example worm compost bin, and trivia!

ad_creative_link_descriptions:
The average American family spends $1,500 a year on food that doesn’t get eaten. You can learn to save money and the environment in your home and community at the FREE Save the Food Fest.

The Save the Food Fest will be hosted by WSU Snohomish County Extension’s Sustainable Community Stewards, Agricultural Program, and SNAP-ED Program from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. on Saturday, April 5th at the Rosehill Community Center, located at 304 Lincoln Ave Ste 108.

The event is free and open to all Snohomish County residents, though a donation to the Mukilteo Food Bank is highly encouraged.

Event details:

Visitors can attend two workshops and participate in a host of educational activities during the event.
* 11 a.m. – 12 p.m. Rosy Smit, Agricultural Coordinator for WSU Snohomish County Extension, will teach attendees to preserve and store food properly, including a demo of a quick pickling recipe anyone can do.
* 12 p.m. – 1 p.m. Free time to partake in the Fest’s art and recipe contests, worm compost bin, kitchen supplies raffle, and trivia.
* 1 p.m. – 2 p.m. Kellee Byard, program coordinator for the Sustainable Community Stewards, will explain how to create a waste-free kitchen at home and cooking with stems and ends to make delicious food from unexpected sources.
* 2 p.m. – 3 p.m. Do any activities you didn’t have time for earlier as the event wraps up and contest/raffle winners are announced

This event supports the state of Washington’s goal to cut food waste in half by 2030. 

“Research shows that people don’t like to waste food—and that learning a few simple strategies can help them to achieve that. This saves money, protects the environment, and helps to ensure that everyone in our communities has access to healthy food,” from Jade Monroe, lead for the Washington State Food Center.

Questions? Reach out to Program Coordinator Kellee Byard at kellee.byard@wsu.edu or call 425-357-6027. Learn more about Food Waste Prevention Week on their website https://foodwastepreventionweek.com . Learn more about Washington's food waste prevention efforts at https://usefoodwell.org

STAY HYDRATED! 🥤💧 With this message, we put our new water dispensers into operation last week.

By switching from reusable water bottles to water dispensers, we want to take the next small step in the right direction on our way to becoming a more sustainable company. In doing so, we are making a contribution to environmental protection and also promoting the health of our colleagues. The new water dispensers are designed to encourage our colleagues to stay hydrated at work.

By using the water dispensers, we are not only reducing a small part of our CO2 emissions, we are also conserving resources. The dispensers use filtered tap water, which is available directly on site, so there is no need to produce and transport bottled water. 🌍💚

🚨 The Supreme Court Might Bring Back CONVERSION THERAPY?! 🚨

The harmful, debunked practice that falsely claims it can change someone’s sexual orientation or gender identity is under threat of making a comeback because the Supreme Court just agreed to hear a case that could overturn state bans.

📢 Every major mental health organization has condemned conversion therapy as junk science that leads to higher rates of depression, anxiety, and even suicidal ideation.

With a Master’s in Psychology specializing in gender & sexual fluidity and graduate certificate in marriage and family therapy, Kyle can tell you firsthand: this is NOT therapy. This is harm. Why would we bring back something that has been PROVEN to hurt people? 

Sound off in the comments 👇 

#lgbtq #conversiontherapy  #TransRightsAreHumanRights #transrights #equality

ad_text:
🗳️ FINAL WEEK FOR EARLY VOTING 
Let’s build a La Crosse that works for everyone.

I’m Aron Newberry, and I’m running to represent District 9 on the La Crosse City Council. I’m honored to be endorsed by a broad coalition of organizations that believe in equity, service, and real leadership:

💥 Who’s Endorsing Aron Newberry and Why:
🔬 314 Action – supports pro-science, data-driven candidates with a background in public service and a commitment to evidence-based decision-making.

🌎 Citizen Action of Wisconsin – backs bold progressive candidates who fight for healthcare access, climate justice, and economic equity.

🗳️ La Crosse County Democratic Party – endorses candidates grounded in community values, ready to lead with integrity and vision.

📈 Wisconsin Progress – recruits and trains candidates who will stand up for working families and progressive change in local government.

🛠️ Wisconsin AFL-CIO – supports candidates who fight for workers’ rights, fair wages, and strong union protections.

🎖️ VoteVets – champions veteran leaders committed to service beyond the uniform, advocating for smart policy and inclusive communities.

🏡 Working Families Party – endorses candidates who fight for affordable housing, livable wages, and racial and economic justice.

🕌 Wisconsin Muslim Civic Alliance – supports candidates who stand for inclusion, equity, and the protection of civil rights for all people.

What do they all have in common?
They believe in leadership that shows up, listens, and delivers.
They sought me out because I’ve spent my career removing barriers to care, standing up for the most vulnerable, and building partnerships to solve real problems.

✅ Endorsed.
📍 Rooted in District 9.
💼 Proven public service.
🏙️ Ready to lead.

🗳️ Vote early this week or on Election Day—Tuesday, April 2.
Let’s build a La Crosse rooted in Service. Leadership. Community.

🔗 Learn more: linkedin.com/in/newberry54601
#VoteNewberry #District9 #EarlyVoting #LaCrosseForward #EndorsedToLea

ad_text:
TOP STORY: New research revealing high levels of mercury in Texas bay raises alarms about dredging for oil tankers 

A scientist’s research showing high levels of mercury in seafood and sediment in a Texas bay is raising concerns that a proposed dredging project to benefit an expanding oil terminal would release more mercury into the bay. 

The proposed expansion of the Seahawk Oil Terminal in Point Comfort is part of an oil export boom along the Gulf Coast that has unfolded since Congress in 2015 reversed a ban on U.S. crude oil exports. The Port Lavaca project is one of 15 oil terminal projects across the country – including nine new export terminals planned, five expansions, and one under construction, according to public records compiled in the Oil & Gas Watch database. 

Jessica Dutton, a marine toxicologist and associate professor of biology at Texas State University, described her research findings about mercury in Lavaca Bay during an August 22 community meeting in Port Lavaca. 

Dutton's research found high levels of the neurotoxin not only in sediment at the bottom of Lavaca Bay, located about two hours southwest of Houston, but also in nearly half of some fish species caught in part of the estuary. 

Her study comes in the context of a massive proposed U.S. Army Corps of Engineers dredging project to widen, lengthen, and deepen a shipping channel that cuts through the bay to allow larger and more heavily loaded oil tankers to access the terminal. Local fishermen and environmental advocates are concerned that dredging will stir up mercury in the bay and allow it to accumulate in fish, shrimp, crabs, oysters, and other wildlife.  

Read the full story at news.oilandgaswatch.org.

🌍✊ Historic Moment for Our Planet! ✊🌍

DAY 1: The final round of @unep negotiations for a Global Plastics Treaty is now underway! This is a pivotal moment in the fight against plastic pollution. Environmental and civil society organizations and frontline, fenceline and Indigenous experts have come together to demand a strong and effective global framework to tackle the growing crisis.

The Pacific Environment team is here on the ground in Busan, Republic of Korea advocating for an ambitious treaty with legally binding measures for curbing plastic production, a monumental step toward a more sustainable future for our planet. 🌱

Help us keep pressure on global leaders to take bold action and make real change! The time to act is NOW. 🌎

ad_text:
The education challenges facing Africa are significant, but so is the continent's potential. Reports show that millions of children lack equitable access to quality education, with the number of out-of-school children still on the rise. With low foundational learning outcomes and a massive funding gap, there is an urgent need for collective action to unlock Africa's human potential.

At Chery Group, we believe education is the most powerful tool for sustainable change. We’re committed to moving beyond words—taking real action.

On August 21st, Chery Group will host the Champion for Education in Africa event, uniting governments, NGOs, and industry leaders to build partnerships and drive lasting impact for every child.

This is not just an event, but a commitment to a shared future. As Chery Group’s fastest-growing personalized brand, OMODA & JAECOO also upholds strong social responsibility, empowering change together.

#CheryGroupForAfrica #PoweringTomorrowThroughEducation #ChampionForEducationInAfrica #OMODAJAECOO

Agri-Pulse Newsmakers: Nov. 1, 2024 

Donald Trump won the presidency and the GOP won control of the Senate this week. Some House race votes are still being tallied.

We sat down with two former agriculture secretaries, Dan Glickman from the Clinton administration and Mike Johanns from the Bush administration, to discuss what could be ahead regarding trade and immigration policy, as well as the possible role of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in the Trump administration.

ad_text: THIS is what a strong sense of community is all about: coming together to support fellow Mainers in need. It takes all of us to ensure food insecurity doesn’t remain a top concern for Maine families. Through our county’s charitable arm, partnerships with the Good Shepherd Food Bank, local grocery stores & restaurants, and the generosity of community members, the York County Commission will ensure our regional food pantry will continue to operate and feed our neighbors.

Lawmakers Warn Forced Electrification Could Harm Electric Grid 🚨 

Rep. Lloyd Smucker and others caution that the EPA’s "Clean Power Plan 2.0" might: 
- Endanger grid reliability for 65 million Americans from NJ to NC. 
- Force closure of existing power plants, leading to power rationing and higher costs. 
- Risk of rolling blackouts and increases in already-high energy costs. 

These risks highlight the need for diverse energy sources like Bioheat® fuel to ensure reliable, affordable power for all. Click below to read more:

🌎✨ Ready to lead the change in your community?

Cool OC’s Youth Climate Leadership Program is now accepting applications for Summer 2025! Middle and high school students in Buena Park, Fullerton, and nearby areas—this is your chance to build your community leadership skills for real change. 💪🌱

✅ 8 hours of interactive training (virtual or in-person)
✅ Independent projects to put your learning into action
✅ Graduation celebration + certificate of completion
✅ Choose your session: July or August!

Whether you’re passionate about clean energy, climate action, or community resilience, this program gives you the tools to lead.

DEADLINE: May 23rd, 11:59pm.

🎉 Spots are limited—apply now! Link is in bio or visit www.cooloc.org/youth
📧 Questions? email programs@cooloc.org

#CoolOC #ClimateLeaders #YouthLeadership #ClimateAction #BuenaPark #Fullerton #NorthOC #StudentLeaders #Sustainability #FutureChangemakers

ad_text:
Over a century after its discovery, the Mount Lyell shrew has finally been photographed alive for the first time. Until last October, the Mount Lyell Shrew was the ONLY known mammal species in California that had never been photographed alive.

Last fall, California Creator for Nature Vishal Subramanyan visited the Eastern Sierra Nevada to study and document this animal. Despite this shrew being a species of special concern, scientists know next to nothing about it. They’re thought to be highly vulnerable to climate change impacts due to their dependence on high altitude habitats that are quickly disappearing.

To find this animal, Vishal and his colleagues worked around the clock, staying up through the night to maintain and monitor traps. They successfully found and photographed 5 individuals, and learned valuable ecological information about the species—and its neighbors! Careful planning, collaboration with UC Berkeley’s Museum of Vertebrate Zoology, and genetic testing at Cal Academy  helped confirm the identification of the species.
bert_topic_keywords: extinction, species, wildlife, petaluma, habitat, endangered, poaching, endangered species, animals, conservation

ad_text:
A Ban on Large Modern Farms Was Stopped in Sonoma County, California

In November 2024, Sonoma County residents voted against a proposed ban on large modern farms. Created by Direct Action Everywhere (DxE), an anti-animal agriculture group known for trespassing on private agribusinesses and stealing livestock and poultry, Measure J threatened to permanently ban most modern farms and animal husbandry practices in Sonoma County.

Local farmers came together with early voting on the ban, showing an 85% “no” vote on Measure J, according to Dayna Ghirardelli, executive director of the Sonoma County Farm Bureau, in a KQED press release. Support also came from outside the local farming community, with campaign spending on Measure J reaching $2 million.

At AGPROfessionals, we also stepped up to advocate for our animal agriculture clients in response to the Measure J ban as well as the proposed bans in Denver, Colorado. Our communications team produced original articles and boosted them on our social media outlets. We also shared articles and notices produced by livestock association groups and other partners. Over the last number of months, our efforts reached tens of thousands of voters in each demographic area.

Measure J Would Have Impacted All of Animal Agriculture in the County

According to the Sonoma County Voter Information Guide, animals included in the Measure J ballot initiative were cattle, mature dairy cattle, veal calves, swine, horses, sheep, laying hens, broilers, and ducks, making it clear that no animal agribusiness in Sonoma County would have been exempt. If Measure J were to have passed, all large modern farms in Sonoma County would need to be “phased out,” and future developments of large modern farms would be “prohibited.” Current operations would have had three years to “phase-out” or meet Best Management Practices set by the county Agricultural Commissioner. Failure to cease or comply within the three years would have resulted in fines starting at $1,000 for the first day, $5,000 for the second day, and $10,000 every day in “violation” thereafter. In addition to fines, non-complying large modern farms would have also faced other enforcement available by law and at the discretion of the Agricultural Commissioner.

Family Farms Would Have Been Impacted

Opponents of the ban pointed out that the bill targeted multi-generational farming families in Sonoma County. According to an American Farm Bureau fact sheet about agriculture and food, in 2022, 95% of all United States farms are family owned.

If it passed, Measure J would have shut down large modern farms housing 89,000 cattle, 13,508 sheep, 2,935 hogs, 4,362 goats, and 225,496 laying hens, pullets, and broilers, according to the 2023 Sonoma County Crop Report.

Measure J Proponents Used Disinformation to Sway Voters

The verbiage on the ordinance claimed only “industrialized factory farms” would be prohibited and not “family farms.” This claim by the writers of Measure J was wholly disingenuous and misleading. Measure J proponents also claimed large modern farms were “major polluters and the breeding ground for diseases.” This statement was also false, as Sonoma County farmers are held to high animal welfare standards and environmental regulatory compliance. Family farms in Sonoma County use best practices to protect water quality and other environmental impacts. The coalition opposing Measure J ensured voters were aware that “Sonoma County’s farms are among the most progressive in the nation, setting high standards in sustainability and animal welfare. Our farmers are leaders in animal care, carbon sequestration, and environmental stewardship, practices that will be undermined by Measure J.”

The Group Behind Measure J – Was Also Behind the Slaughterhouse and Fur Bans in Denver

Founded in 2013 by Wayne Hsuing, a lawyer who subscribes to animal rights and environmental extremist ideology, Direct Action Everywhere (DxE) engages in extreme tactics to attack animal agriculture. An article by Protect The Harvest outlined some of the tactics used by DxE, including committing crimes such as theft of animals, which they call “open rescue,” and focusing on small organic farms that do not have the resources to fight back against DxE activities. DxE’s “open rescue” policy includes breaking into and stealing animals from farms and ranches, which risks the health and welfare of the livestock they steal and the livestock on the premises. According to the same article, the organization also targets small organic farms to further its “all animal agriculture is abuse” narrative.

Pro-Animal Future (PAF) is an anti-animal agriculture group connected with DxE. Based out of Denver, Colorado, PAF is a sub-group of the Phauna Foundation, a 501 c (3). Its website states that the organization is “known in the animal rights community for its studies on public attitudes regarding the use of animals for food.” According to an article by Protect The Harvest, PAF used its influence to further its vegan agenda in the Denver area to get similar initiatives to Measure J on the ballot. The ballot initiatives targeted animal processing facilities and the sale of fur products in Denver city and county.

The Ultimate in Sustainability and Animal Welfare

Today’s farmers and ranchers operate on very thin margins. Animals that are stressed or unhealthy do not produce well. Therefore, animal welfare and efficiency are top priorities for producers. With this in mind, university researchers and livestock veterinarians have developed modern farming practices in conjunction with producers who have generations of practical knowledge and experience. The practices employed by large modern farms help ensure the animals' health and well-being, reduce inputs, and make the most of land, water, and other resources.

Not only are large modern farms, primarily owned by families, feeding the country, but more and more are doing so utilizing sustainable energy. Some examples of the use of renewable energy on farms and ranches include geothermal, solar panels, windmills, hydro systems, and methane digesters. In 2022, 153,101 farms and ranches used renewable energy. This is a 167% increase from 2012 when only 57,299 farms used on-farm renewable energy practices.

Large Modern Farms Support Local Economies

The latest statistics from the American Farm Bureau Federation show that there are 1.9 million farms and ranches in the US, comprising less than 2% of the population. Family-run operations account for more than 95% of total agricultural production value. One large modern farm, on average, feeds 169 people in the U.S. and abroad annually.

In Sonoma County, large modern farms support the local, state, and national economy by adding tens of millions of dollars in revenue each year. According to the 2023 Sonoma County Crop Report, the livestock industry in Sonoma County contributed $39,489,100 to the local economy in 2023.

Looking Toward the Future

In her article about the recent ballot initiative in Sonoma County, Hannah Weeman Thompson from Animal Agriculture Alliance emphasized the importance of staying abreast of threats to animal agriculture and turning out to vote “no” when these initiatives appear on the ballot. We must stand up against groups like DxE and PAF and stop bans on large modern farms and slaughterhouses from gaining traction. “Losses today are unlikely to deter activists,” Weeman said, “as they believe ‘even if none of them pass, it’s going to set a precedent, we’ve already won by getting these on the ballot.’”

Thankfully, Measure J did not pass, and neither did the bans in Denver. However, despite this defeat at the polls, the threat to large modern farms is far from over. The groups vying to end animal agriculture are motivated by their ideology and they have a long game.

Links to references can be found in this article on our website

In this episode of American Potential, host David From sits down with Congressman August Pfluger—Chairman of the Republican Study Committee, decorated Air Force veteran, and proud seventh-generation Texan—for an in-depth conversation on the path forward for America's economy, energy independence, and national security.

Congressman Pfluger discusses why predictability in the tax code is essential for families, entrepreneurs, and innovators alike. As the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act faces expiration, Pfluger makes the case for why extending—and ideally making permanent—those provisions is not just a matter of policy, but a critical step to preserve America's competitive edge and avoid economic backsliding.

He also shares how the Republican Study Committee, now the largest conservative caucus in Congress with 189 members, is working to restore fiscal discipline, protect family-owned farms from devastating inheritance taxes, and ensure American businesses have the confidence to invest and grow.

The conversation dives into the real-world impact of smart tax policy, using examples like a small business in rural Ohio that was able to give employees a 40% raise thanks to the Trump tax cuts. Congressman Pfluger also breaks down the growing demand for domestic energy production—driven in part by rising electricity needs from data centers and AI—and why energy security is inseparable from national security.

Representing one of the largest and most quintessentially Texan districts in the country, Congressman Pfluger shares what makes his constituents special, why the Permian Basin is a story of American innovation, and what it means to fight for the freedom and opportunity they hold dear.

This episode is a must-listen for anyone who wants to understand how principled leadership, clear policy direction, and bold vision can help secure a stronger future for all Americans.
bert_topic_keywords: green new, new deal, tax cuts, big beautiful, cuts, trillion, medicaid, debt, deal, biden green

From the Wisconsin State Journal:  
Julia Henley-- District 41
 

Julia Henley, a Democrat from Blue River, wants to protect the beauty of the Driftless Area while encouraging rural development in sustainable ways. Her background in construction and design, as well as flood relief, will bring knowledge and ideas to state debates over regulation. Henley faults state leaders for “starving” schools in the district, which includes Wisconsin Dells, Reedsburg and Richland Center. She also faults Republicans who control the Legislature for failing to take federal Medicaid dollars, which she says could help keep more rural hospitals open. Julia Henley-- District 41
 

Julia Henley, a Democrat from Blue River, wants to protect the beauty of the Driftless Area while encouraging rural development in sustainable ways. Her background in construction and design, as well as flood relief, will bring knowledge and ideas to state debates over regulation. Henley faults state leaders for “starving” schools in the district, which includes Wisconsin Dells, Reedsburg and Richland Center. She also faults Republicans who control the Legislature for failing to take federal Medicaid dollars, which she says could help keep more rural hospitals open. Henley favors abortion rights, unlike her opponent, Rep. Tony Kurtz, R-Wonewoc, who didn’t meet with our editorial board. ad_text:
Dear Butler County and District 3 Residents,

I would like to address some concerns regarding RED PAC and the endorsement I received from them. It appears there is some misunderstanding or misinformation circulating, and I want to take this opportunity to clarify the situation. I encourage you to read through this message to understand the truth.

Recently, my opponent running for District 3 made statements that are completely false and misleading. You deserve transparency, and I have consistently maintained an open and honest approach throughout my campaign. Unlike my opponent, I believe in earning your trust and vote through integrity, not by spreading falsehoods. 

My opponent has claimed that I support large-scale wind and solar farms, which is far from the truth. He also claimed to have thoroughly researched RED PAC and their endorsement, but it seems he did not dig deep enough. RED PAC, the Rural Economic Development Political Action Committee, is led by conservative Republicans who support other conservative Republicans in local, state, and national races. Their core values include protecting private property rights, increasing government transparency, fostering common-sense solutions, and promoting rural economic development—values I strongly share, which is why I was honored to accept their endorsement.

During my interview with RED PAC, we discussed important issues such as public safety, government accountability and transparency, private property rights, and economic development. Towards the end of the discussion, we addressed wind and solar energy. I made it clear that I DO NOT support large-scale solar or wind farms. However, I believe that landowners, farmers, and homeowners should have the right to use solar or wind energy on their property. It's their right, and they should be able to do as they wish with their land. I fully support our residents and property owners making decisions that help them provide for their families, bring jobs to the county, and contribute to our county's budget. But let me be very clear—I DO NOT support large-scale wind or solar farms.

My opponent’s claim that RED PAC supports large-scale wind and solar farms is incorrect and misleading. While RED PAC has endorsed some conservative candidates who support such projects, they have also supported candidates who oppose them and those from counties with outright bans on wind and solar farms. For instance, RED PAC endorsed two candidates in Harvey County, which has a total ban on wind and solar farms. These candidates have clearly stated they will not remove these bans. Similarly, RED PAC endorsed a candidate in Sedgwick County who, like me, opposes large-scale wind and solar farms. This demonstrates that RED PAC’s endorsement is based on shared values, not specific stances on wind and solar farms, as my opponent falsely claims.

It is also important to address the misinformation being spread by one of my opponent’s supporters, who claims that I received nearly $21,000 from RED PAC. This is entirely untrue. I have not received any financial contributions from RED PAC; the only thing I received was their endorsement. RED PAC independently decided to spend money on mailers to support my campaign. I had no control over their spending, as they operate independently. Their financial report to the state is separate from my campaign finances, as I did not receive any money from them. And I have not received any Money from any other large organizations, or companies. I’d also like to point out that this supporter is so interested in me, my life, my wife and my campaign and claims to know everything should know that RED PACs endorsement is not considered an In-Kind Contribution to my Campaign Per The State of Kansas Governmental Ethics Commission it is an individual contribution/expenditure since I had no control over what they were contributing to my campaign. Because again they are their own entity and I only received their endorsement not any money. This clearly goes to show this supporter is not very educated but continues to try and make me look bad any way they can. Me and my Wife have put in almost $8000.00 of our own hard worked money into my campaign to make it possible so I can hopefully serve you all with Respect, Honor and Integrity. 

I would like to point out that Butler County currently has a wind farm spanning over 7,000 acres. I do not support projects of this size; however, since the farm is already established, there is little that can be done about it now. They pay their taxes and maintain the area, which is all we can ask for. Butler County also has several smaller solar farms managed by Butler Electric Co-op. I support these small-scale farms because they provide local energy, are well-maintained, and positively impact our residents. I believe that property owners and the county could benefit from smaller-scale projects ranging from 1 to 40 acres. However, I do not agree with the current regulation that allows such projects within 3 miles of communities; I believe the distance should be 10-15 miles from any residential area or community and should be located off county dirt roads, out of sight, so we can continue to enjoy our county’s natural beauty. I want to emphasize again that I DO NOT support large-scale farms.

Throughout this campaign, my opponent has resorted to dishonesty, slander, and a lack of transparency. He has accused me of running a dirty campaign, but I believe correcting misinformation and false accusations is not “slinging mud”; it is about standing by my values of honesty and transparency. I will continue to correct any falsehoods because you deserve to know the truth.

I genuinely love our community and have pledged to serve Butler County with integrity, particularly in ensuring private property rights, maintaining transparency and accountability in governance, being fiscally conservative, Listening to your voices and addressing your concerns, Enhancing Public Safety, Making the way of life better for residents, Finding Solutions to the problems and promoting economic development

If you want change and someone that is going to be honest and transparent with you and listen to your concerns and voices then I would greatly appreciate your vote on or before August 6th so that we can work together to create a brighter future for all of Butler County.
bert_topic_keywords: party, kodel, democratic, young, progressive, laible, leaders deserve, kodel party, steve william, william laible

ad_text:
The South is facing the one of the largest methane gas buildouts in the country.

As utilities regionwide continue to propose new gas plants and gas pipelines that would lock us into yet another fossil fuel for decades, derailing the transition from coal to clean energy.

Methane gas power plants and compressor stations threaten surrounding communities — often communities of color who have already borne the brunt of harm from industrial facilities for decades — with dangerous air pollution.

SELC and our partners are pursuing all legal and policy avenues to stop this gas grab: filing lawsuits against proposed plants and pipelines, challenging utility plans in public utility commission proceedings, advocating for renewable energy alternatives, and working to leverage federal climate action.

Learn more at our link in bio.

OPEN HOUSE SATURDAY 10/5 12-4 AND SUNDAY 10/6 12-5

An Unprecedented Opportunity In A Sold Out Community...

Welcome To Your Dream Home At The Farm In Poway! 
CLICK THE LINK BELOW FOR A FULL PROPERTY SHOWCASE:
http://www.17985standrewsdr.com

Newly built and released in 2023 this exquisite 5-bedroom, 5.5 bathroom residence offers the perfect blend of luxury, comfort, technology, and easy modern living.

This residence is the largest in the "Homestead" enclave and features the highly sought after Lennar "NextGen Suite". 
The "NextGen" suite functions as a "home within a home" offering guests or family a private one bedroom suite including a living room, kitchen, full bathroom and in-unit laundry with a private entrance.

Energy efficiency and technology is at the forefront of this home's design with a fully owned 11 panel state-of-the-art solar system by Sunnova, ensuring lower utility costs and a reduced carbon footprint. Enjoy efficient on demand hot water with a tankless heater and meet everyones temperature preferences with 3 separate climate zones. Control the homes systems at the touch of a button through a series of "smart home" apps.

As you step inside the home, you'll be greeted by a bright and airy open-concept layout that seamlessly connects the living spaces, creating an inviting atmosphere for both family gatherings and entertaining guests. 
The gourmet kitchen is a chef's delight, featuring quartz countertops, stainless steel appliances, and elegant Shaker-style cabinetry.

Enjoy top tier Monogram appliances featuring a 6 burner range and oven along with a separate double oven set that can all be controlled by the Monogram app.

The spacious bedrooms provide tranquil retreats, with en suite bathrooms that feature high-end fixtures and finishes.

A dedicated office space on the main floor offers peaceful work at home experiences and an upstairs flex room provides an additional space for entertaining, studying, and working out.

This home occupies one of the largest and best oriented lots in the entire beautiful master-planned community of The Farm and is situated directly next to the charming native plant and butterfly garden.

The home is adjacent to all of the special amenities of The Farm including incredible hiking trails, picnic areas, playground, community gardens, and dog park. 

You are in close and easy proximity to all the best of Poway including some of the highest rated schools in the county, Lake Poway, shopping, dining, numerous country clubs and golf courses and the esteemed Rancho Bernardo Inn And Spa. 
Enjoy hiking Iron Mountain and nearby equestrian opportunities. 

This home offers the perfect balance of suburban tranquility and modern amenities. 

This community has sold out of other models and this is your very unique and special opportunity to own a new, beautiful, and thoughtfully designed home in what is truly one of the most well appointed new home communities in San Diego.

On Tuesday, August 13, I conducted interviews with Shreveport television stations KTBS-TV and KTAL-TV. I discussed the recent decision to not recommend Bank of America be approved as an authorized fiscal agent in the State of Louisiana per LRS 49:317 and 320. 

This decision was not entered into lightly but was made because there is evidence that Bank of America is deliberately denying banking services to customers and potential customers (de-banking) of religious organizations, gun manufacturers, fossil fuel producers and others based simply on their political perspectives and activities, not because of any bank policy or law violations.

ad_text:
🖤 Silent Screams of an American Whale
Only ~50 Rice’s whales remain in the world.
They live in a single, unprotected stretch of the Gulf of Mexico.

Every day, they are threatened by:
🚢 Ship collisions
⚙️ Oil drilling
🔊 Noise pollution
🌊 Plastic & toxic runoff

NOAA has yet to designate their habitat as critical.
But silence is no longer an option.

📣 Demand a protected zone.
🐋 Tag @NOAA
📎 Sign the petition:

https://www.change.org/p/designate-noaa-critical-habitat-for-rice-s-whales

We are proud to receive the Top Score award for the fourth consecutive year from the Sustainable Furnishings Council and National Wildlife Federation!  
 
Minted Space’s mission is to improve access to and affordability of elevated, eco-friendly products.  We scout sustainably-sourced, responsibly made products for our online store and eco-luxe interior design projects.
 
The Wood Furniture Scorecard is an important initiative in the furniture industry.  It encourages transparency and accountability for how wood products are sourced. "'These jobs that are going to be created, many will be union jobs,' [Robert Joerg, director of government affairs for the Michigan Laborers District Council], said. 'For the construction industry, you’re looking at healthcare, a pension—which is quite rare today…'"

"'It’s exciting to have folks in our state government that are thinking 20, 30, and 40 years into the future so we don’t repeat the mistakes of the past, and so that workers aren’t being left behind,' Joerg said. 'These workers are going to have well-paying, stable careers for decades to come.'"

What are energy independence and security, and how can incorporating locally-generated renewable energy help bolster these in our community and across the country?

According to the @[100068384958383:2048:U.S. Department of Energy],  energy independence means a nation doesn’t need to import energy to meet its needs, and security means that there is enough energy available and that the infrastructure needed to distribute it is protected from vulnerabilities. By adding locally-generated energy to the electrical grid, renewables add diversity that makes the grid more flexible and more secure. 💪

If you support a more secure and reliable electric grid, sign a letter of support for Clear Mountain Energy Center today.

St. Clair County Residents: The Central Alabama Climate Action Plan team needs your help to create a Plan that addresses the current and future impacts of climate change within the Central Alabama region. The Plan is a roadmap for reaching a 2050 greenhouse gas reduction goal. With the Plan, our region will continue to develop as a vibrant and sustainable area for current and future generations.

Join us for a Community Engagement Meeting about climate change on Thursday, May 15, from 5:30 to 7:00 pm at the Pell City Center for Education and the Performing Arts (📍 25 Williamson Dr; Pell City, AL 35125). 

Your participation and input will be key. This plan is designed to be a community-driven plan, and we want to make sure to have as many opportunities as possible to engage with everyone across the region. 

Visit our website to RSVP and learn more: https://centralalabamaclimate.com/#tab-64259

A SPECIAL 7/10 WITH FRENCHY CANNOLI

We are over the top excited to announce a special screening and Oregon premier of the movie Frenchy Dreams of Hashish, a documentary by Jake Remington. 

FRENCHY DREAMS OF HASHISH SHOWCASES MASTER HASHISHIN FRENCHY CANNOLI’S LIFELONG PURSUIT OF PERFECT HASHISH AND HIGHLIGHTS THE ETHOS OF SUSTAINABLE, SUN-GROWN CANNABIS.

During filming, in addition to intense wildfires, California’s small cannabis farmers experienced economic assassination at the hands of government and corporate interests. This formed the context of the film in which Frenchy and the farmers’ fight for quality, and survival, intensified. Through the lens of Frenchy Cannoli’s life, this documentary showcases the importance of craft, quality, and perseverance. It also creates a conversation around the global topics of climate change, health care, sustainability, and corporatocracy.

Join us as we pay respect to the master hash maker, Frenchy Cannoli on the official processor holiday, 7/10, in Portland, Oregon. Showtime is at 7:10pm, doors at 6:15PM

Greater Wythe. Aberdeen. Briarwood Terrace.
These are Hampton’s pilot communities for this vital initiative.
Your voice is needed — come help shape the future of our neighborhoods and our city.

🗣 Our Voice. Our Neighborhood. Our Future.

🗓 Wednesday, July 30 | 🕠 5:30 PM–7:30 PM
📍 Boys & Girls Club – 1815 Shell Rd, Hampton, VA

💬 This is more than an event. It’s a conversation.
Join City Hall, Hampton Innovation, and your neighbors for a community feedback session that puts your voice at the center of real decision-making.

🌍 Explore “The Wall Walk” — an interactive research exhibit that features local stories, resident input, and critical challenges around housing, flooding, and climate resilience.

👥 Why You Should Come:
 • Walk through Hampton’s new research plan
 • Learn what’s being done to address housing + flooding
 • Share your experiences, concerns, and ideas
 • Help guide next steps that directly impact your neighborhood

🎟 FREE to attend
🍪 Light refreshments served
📝 Register here: https://form.jotform.com/251983192760060

⸻

This is a true partnership.
Residents + City + Hampton Innovation = A stronger, more resilient Hampton.

📩 Questions?
Demetrious.Keilu@hampton.gov
Chad.Sanders@hampton.gov

ad_text:
The truth about industrial farming is hard to swallow. For decades, we’ve been led to believe that mass-produced food is the key to feeding the world’s growing population. But the reality is stark: industrial farming is destroying us.

It’s destroying our soil, stripping it of its nutrients and leaving it barren and lifeless. It’s destroying our health, pumping our bodies full of toxic chemicals and processed foods that are linked to chronic diseases. And it’s destroying our planet, contributing to climate change, deforestation, and the loss of biodiversity.

But there’s hope. There’s a movement growing, a movement that’s rooted in the connection between healthy soil, healthy humans, and healthy animals.

On February 15th at 5pm, we invite you to join us for an exclusive viewing of Common Ground, a powerful documentary that exposes the truth about industrial farming and offers a solution.
 
So join us on February 15th at 5pm for this exclusive viewing of Common Ground. Let’s come together to create a better future for our planet, our health, and our communities. 

#CommonGround #RegenerativeAgriculture #HealthySoilHealthyHumans
bert_topic_keywords: dairy, food, soil, milk, meat, agriculture, soil health, farming, cocoa, regenerative

ad_text:
Did you know that the Arctic is heating up 4x faster than the rest of the planet! 🥶 

This rapid warming is causing dramatic changes, like melting sea ice and shifting weather patterns. ❄️🌧️ A major culprit? Rising CO2 levels from human activities. 

But here's the good news: BLOOM is tackling this challenge on two fronts!  First, by removing harmful algae blooms from ecosystems, and second, by using that algae to create sustainable materials. This process locks away CO2 that would otherwise be released into the atmosphere, helping to combat climate change. 

Learn more about how BLOOM is making a difference by visiting our website at www.bloommaterials.com!

#MadeWithBLOOM #algae #cleanwater #cleanair #oceancleanup #ClimateChange #ArcticWarming

ad_creative_link_descriptions:
Have you or a loved one struggled to find a therapist in Illinois who takes your insurance? Many of us are unable to find mental health care providers within our insurance networks because the insurance companies refuse to pay them fairly. We need mental health care, and our providers deserve to be compensated fairly for their expertise and skills.

The Improve Network Adequacy and Access for Behavioral Health Bill (HB1085) will require private insurers to pay therapists more adequately and make critical mental health care available to more Illinoisans. Send a letter to your legislator, urging them to pass this bill now!,Have you or a loved one struggled to find a therapist in Illinois who takes your insurance? We need mental health care, and our providers deserve to be compensated fairly for their expertise and skills.

The Improve Network Adequacy and Access for Behavioral Health Bill (HB1085) will require private insurers to pay therapists more adequately and make critical mental health care available to more Illinoisans. Send a letter to your legislator, urging them to pass this bill now!

To support women-owned MSMEs in South and Southeast Asia, SecondMuse Capital, with the support of @visa Foundation, has a designed a new financial mechanism called the "Revolving Fund". It aims to deploy $18 million and support up to 75 companies over the next 10 years, providing both capital and technical assistance. 

In this article, our Programs Director, Laura Benns, shares how the fund continuously recycles capital and integrates the plastic credits marketplace as a key innovation in making the Revolving Fund self-sustaining or evergreen in nature. 

Click the link in our bio to learn more about how the Revolving Fund can create change! 🌱

Attention, Millbrae community members! Sign up to join a free, hands-on Rain Barrel Installation Workshop at Green Hills Elementary School, this coming Monday, November 18 at 9 am!
